title: Tynsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, charged with murder after remains found under his Tyngsborough garage are believed to be a missing Lowell woman.
The missing woman, Jill T. Kloppenburg, disappeared in January 2025; Sullivan is also charged with improper disposal of human remains.
Police located the remains using radar, finding them buried in a patched area of Sullivan's garage.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman from Houston, Texas, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise funds for Massachusetts General Hospital.
Felterman is honoring her son Charles, who has Dup15q syndrome, and his doctor, Ronald Thibert at MGH.
Dr. Thibert has been treating Charles for epilepsy for 14 years, significantly improving his quality of life.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers, 35, from Belmont is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in support of Caring for a Cure.
Ben's wife, Coney Flowers, survived Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia and a successful bone-marrow transplant after 70 days in the hospital.
Caring for a Cure, supported by Flowers, aids patients with services like childcare and grocery assistance during treatment.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie, 33, from Southampton, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass General's pediatric cancer team.
Labrie participates under the motto 'I Run For Them,' honoring pediatric cancer patients and raising donations through her run.
Her motivation stems from personal ties to pediatric cancer, with dedications to specific children and care recipients.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Out of Body' premieres on Netflix on April 13.
The documentary chronicles Kahan's life, creative journey, and family over a year and a half.
Noah Kahan's fourth album 'The Great Divide' releases on April 24.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ as a 'special contributor' after being laid off last fall.
Keller re-launches the 'Keller At Large' interview show with Gov. Maura Healey as the first guest.
Keller will continue his weekly MASSterList column and contribute to Boston magazine.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are considering legislation to end the biannual clock change, moving potentially to permanent Atlantic Standard Time.
A Boston.com poll shows 83% of over 470 respondents favor ending the clock change, citing health and routine disruptions.
The proposed change would require at least two other New England states to adopt the same time system for implementation.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham was arrested after driving 118 mph and crashing a 2014 Chrysler 300 in NH.
The teen led police through several towns before crashing into a retaining wall in Stratham, New Hampshire.
The driver faces felony charges for reckless conduct and misdemeanors, including aggravated DUI and drug possession.

title: The ‘World’s Shortest St. Paddy’s Day Parade’ is still New England’s nuttiest St. Pat’s fever dream
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: The 'World's Shortest St. Paddy's Day Parade' in Adamsville, Rhode Island, measures 89 feet and attracts around 2,000 attendees.
Founded by the Kinnane Brothers, the parade features costumes, music, and floats, benefiting local food banks with over $10K raised.
A friendly rivalry with Hot Springs, Arkansas, questions parade length claims, with a documentary exploring this unique St. Patrick's event.

title: Massachusetts ER doctors say long wait times reflect a health system under strain
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts emergency room doctors report long wait times due to hospital bed shortages and staffing issues.
Patients face average ER stays of 189 minutes, with some experiencing delays of 10-30 hours, according to doctors.
ER overcrowding worsened post-COVID-19 as aging patients and primary care shortages increased hospital system strain.
